key_points: A force is a push or a pull acting on an object
Force can change the shape, speed, or direction of an object
Forces can act through contact or without contact
Contact forces include muscular force and friction
Non-contact forces include gravitational force, magnetic force, and electrostatic force
examples: Pushing a door to open it
Pulling a bucket from a well
An apple falling from a tree due to gravity
formulas: Force = Push or Pull acting on an object
Gravitational force → Objects fall toward the Earth due to gravity
Friction → Force that opposes motion between two surfaces
exam_tips: Remember the difference between contact and non-contact forces
Use everyday examples when explaining forces
Mention effects of force such as change in speed or shape
Revise types of forces with examples
